```python
import pytest

def celsius_to_fahrenheit(c: float) -> float:
    return c * 9/5 + 32

# Happy path tests: Standard conversions with expected results
@pytest.mark.parametrize("celsius, expected_fahrenheit", [
    (0, 32),      # Freezing point
    (100, 212),   # Boiling point
    (25, 77),     # Room temperature
    (37, 98.6),   # Body temperature
])
def test_celsius_to_fahrenheit_happy_path(celsius, expected_fahrenheit):
    result = celsius_to_fahrenheit(celsius)
    assert result == expected_fahrenheit

# Edge cases: Boundary values, negatives, large/small numbers
@pytest.mark.parametrize("celsius, expected_fahrenheit", [
    (-40, -40),   # Point where Celsius and Fahrenheit are equal
    (-273.15, -459.67),  # Absolute zero (approximate)
    (1000, 1832), # Large positive number
    (-1000, -1768), # Large negative number
    (0.0, 32.0),  # Zero as float
    (1e-6, 32.0000018),  # Very small positive (test precision)
    (-1e-6, 31.9999982), # Very small negative (test precision)
])
def test_celsius_to_fahrenheit_edge_cases(celsius, expected_fahrenheit):
    result = celsius_to_fahrenheit(celsius)
    assert result == pytest.approx(expected_fahrenheit, rel=1e-5)  # Allow small floating-point tolerance

# Error cases: Invalid inputs that should raise exceptions
@pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_input", [
    ("string",),   # String input
    (None,),       # None input
    ([],),         # List input
    ({},),         # Dict input
])
def test_celsius_to_fahrenheit_error_cases(invalid_input):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        celsius_to_fahrenheit(invalid_input)
```
